---
title: "Setup"
---

Five minutes of setup and you're ready for the whole workshop. If you fall behind on any install, you can still follow along — every result is shown on this site.

## 1. Install Claude Code

Claude Code is Anthropic's agentic coding tool that runs in your terminal.

```bash
# macOS / Linux
curl -fsSL https://claude.com/install.sh | bash
```

Then start it and sign in:

```bash
claude
```

See the [official docs](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/overview) for other install methods (npm, IDE extensions, the desktop app).

## 2. Get the workshop repo

```bash
git clone https://github.com/crazyhottommy/claude_code_RNAseq_workshop.git
cd claude_code_RNAseq_workshop
```

The repo already contains the **salmon `quant.sf` files** for all four samples in `data/salmon/`, so you can start analyzing immediately — no large downloads.

```
data/
├── salmon/
│   ├── Normoxia_sgCTRL_1/quant.sf
│   ├── Normoxia_sgCTRL_2/quant.sf
│   ├── Hypoxia_sgCTRL_1/quant.sf
│   └── Hypoxia_sgCTRL_2/quant.sf
├── samples.csv          # which sample is which condition
├── tx2gene.csv          # transcript → gene
└── gene_name_map.csv    # gene id → gene symbol
```

## 3. Install the R / Bioconductor packages

The downstream analysis is in R. Install everything with the helper script:

```bash
Rscript install.R
```

This installs **tximport, DESeq2, clusterProfiler, org.Hs.eg.db** and the tidyverse pieces (dplyr, readr, ggplot2, pheatmap). Don't have R? Get it from [r-project.org](https://www.r-project.org/) (we use R ≥ 4.3).

## 4. (Optional) tools for preprocessing

Only needed if you want to regenerate `quant.sf` from raw FASTQ (covered in [Preprocessing](02-preprocessing.qmd)). Not required for the live session.

```bash
conda env create -f environment.yml   # installs salmon, sra-tools, fastq-dl
conda activate rnaseq
```

## 5. Launch Claude Code in the repo

```bash
claude
```

Because the repo ships a [`CLAUDE.md`](https://github.com/crazyhottommy/claude_code_RNAseq_workshop/blob/main/CLAUDE.md), Claude Code already knows the dataset, the file layout, and the analysis goal. Try opening with:

::: {.claude-prompt}
[Try this prompt]{.prompt-label}

[Read CLAUDE.md and the files in `data/`, then tell me what this dataset is and what analysis we're about to do.]{.prompt-text}
:::

When it gives a sensible summary, you're set. → On to **[1 · The data](01-the-data.qmd)**.
